title: board split board runtime config from aws env
description: Board runtime should load a separate board env file for board-local defaults, keep aws env focused on AWS and rig defaults, and add configurable cmd_vel raw min and max mapping without changing raw helper semantics.

title: board video sender should inherit resolved AWS credentials from parent
description:
## Problem
 resolves AWS credentials and connects to AWS IoT successfully, but the supervised  child still re-resolves the  profile and fails under systemd with .

## Scope
- Stop relying on child-side profile resolution when the parent already has working credentials.
- Pass a resolved credential snapshot from  into the video sender supervisor/child.
- Keep standalone  support for manual runs.
- Add regression tests for supervisor spawn env and direct child startup with explicit credentials.

## Constraints
- Do not change MQTT/shadow contracts.
- Preserve native sender credential injection behavior.

title: board install-service should use EnvironmentFile defaults again
description:
## Problem
 now bakes resolved AWS env into  lines by default. That overrides  and makes the generated unit harder to reason about and update.

## Scope
- Simplify  so  remains the default source of runtime configuration.
- Keep explicit install-time overrides as  lines only when they were actually passed.
- Preserve  support for the motor stack.
- Update tests/docs to match the simpler model.

## Constraints
- Do not change MQTT/shadow contracts.
- Do not revert unrelated in-progress  changes.

title: board video sender spawn should not depend on implicit inherited AWS env
description:
## Problem
 can connect to AWS IoT, then fail when the supervised  child starts under systemd with . The child currently relies on inherited environment and working directory instead of getting an explicit normalized AWS spawn environment.

## Scope
- Normalize AWS profile/config/credentials env before spawning .
- Pass explicit  to the child so repo-relative config paths resolve consistently.
- Add regression tests around supervisor spawn env/cwd.

## Constraints
- Do not change MQTT/shadow contracts.
- Keep board video startup behavior the same apart from making the child spawn deterministic.
notes: Made board video sender spawn deterministic: normalize AWS_PROFILE/AWS_DEFAULT_PROFILE and credential/config file paths into an explicit child env, and pass explicit cwd to the child subprocess. Added regression tests covering normalized spawn env/cwd. Board tests pass: unittest discover (57 tests). Pending device verification and commit.

title: board service fails video sender when txing profile is not explicitly exported
description:
## Problem
 can start and connect AWS IoT, but  fails with  after recent service/env changes.

## Scope
- Ensure install-service always propagates resolved AWS runtime environment (, , , , ) into systemd unit env, not only when explicit CLI args are passed.
- Keep existing EnvironmentFile behavior.
- Add/adjust tests to lock this behavior.

## Constraints
- No MQTT/shadow contract changes.
- No manual systemctl edits required after .
notes: Patched board::install-service to always emit AWS_REGION/AWS_PROFILE/AWS_DEFAULT_PROFILE/AWS_TXING_PROFILE/AWS_SHARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE/AWS_CONFIG_FILE into board.service Environment lines using resolved values from _project-aws-env; kept board-specific overrides optional. Updated board/tests/test_shadow_control.py assertions and README service docs. Board unit tests pass: unittest discover (55 tests). Pending runtime verification on device and commit.

title: board/ fix lgpio notify pipe path under systemd
description: board runtime fails at startup with xCreatePipe permission/path error for .lgd-nfy0 when lgpio initializes under systemd. Fix by setting a stable writable LG_WD before importing gpiozero LGPIO backend, and document override option.
design: Set default LG_WD=/tmp/txing-lgpio when unset, create dir if needed, then import/use LGPIOFactory. Keep override behavior if LG_WD is explicitly configured. Update board README troubleshooting/runtime notes.

title: board/ add raw left-right motor test helper and just recipe
description:
Add a board-local direct motor test path for bring-up and tuning.

Scope:
- Add a helper/CLI that applies raw left/right motor values directly in the `[-480, 480]` range.
- Reuse the same DRV8835 backend as the main board runtime instead of a separate GPIO path.
- Add a `just` entrypoint for running this manual test path from the repo root.
- The direct test path should bypass MQTT and kinematic mixing; it is for raw backend validation.
design:
Concrete approach:
- Add a small Python entrypoint that accepts raw `left` and `right` values, validates range, applies them through the shared backend, and stops on exit.
- Add a `just board::...` recipe that forwards the raw values into that helper so bring-up does not require composing Python commands manually.
- Keep the command surface focused on raw left/right motor testing rather than simulating a `Twist` payload.

title: board/ add native DRV8835 backend with hardware PWM
description:
Add a repo-owned DRV8835 backend for the board runtime.

Scope:
- Implement the real motor driver adapter used by `CmdVelController`.
- Use hardware PWM for speed and Python-native GPIO line control for direction.
- Keep the stock Pololu shield pin mapping as the default in v1.
- Add runtime configuration for inversion and the hardware/PWM parameters needed to run safely on Raspberry Pi OS.
- Keep shutdown, disconnect, and watchdog stop behavior safe.
design:
Concrete approach:
- Add a motor backend module that exposes the same `setSpeeds(left, right)` shape expected by the existing controller.
- Keep the controller's public/output scale at `[-100, 100]` and introduce an internal mapping layer to raw `[-480, 480]` hardware units.
- Use kernel PWM channels for the speed pins and native Python GPIO for the direction pins; do not depend on WiringPi.
- Ensure close/error handling drives both motors to zero and releases claimed resources cleanly.

title: board/ native DRV8835 motor control and raw test path
description:
Implement real board-side motor control for the Pololu DRV8835 Dual Motor Driver Kit on the txing board runtime.

Plan summary:
- Replace the current stub-only motor backend with a repo-owned native Python backend.
- Do not use the WiringPi-based Pololu Python package.
- Match the Pololu behavior closely by using hardware PWM for the speed channels and native Python GPIO control for the direction channels, following current Raspberry Pi OS guidance.
- Keep the existing MQTT `txing/board/cmd_vel` contract and existing shadow `reported.board.drive.*` contract unchanged.
- Preserve the repo-visible drive scale as signed percent effort `[-100, 100]`, but map that scale internally to the DRV8835 raw range `[-480, 480]`.
- Assume the stock Pololu shield pin mapping in v1.
- Add a manual board-local test path and `just` recipe for setting raw left/right motor values directly in the `[-480, 480]` range.
- Add the required docs, tests, and boot/runtime prerequisites for the PWM path.
design:
Implementation intent:
- Use Linux hardware PWM for the two speed outputs and Python-native GPIO control for the two direction pins.
- Default to the stock Pololu mapping: GPIO12/GPIO13 as PWM outputs and GPIO5/GPIO6 as direction pins.
- Scale percent effort from `CmdVelController` into raw DRV8835 units before hitting hardware.
- Add explicit config for inversion/calibration instead of baking chassis direction assumptions into code.
- Add a small direct motor-test helper and wire it into `just` so raw values can be applied without MQTT.
- Force stop on close/error paths and keep tests hardware-free with fakes around the PWM/GPIO adapters.

title: Handle browser ICE candidate edge cases in KVS video path
description:
Scope:
- web viewer should not signal empty ICE candidates that the native sender cannot parse
- native board sender should tolerate ignorable browser ICE candidate parse failures, including empty candidate payloads and mDNS-obfuscated host candidates, without tearing down the session

Constraints:
- keep current AWS KVS signaling architecture
- keep changes scoped to board and web
- prefer compatibility with modern browser trickle ICE behavior over strict fatal handling

Intended approach:
- filter outgoing viewer ICE candidates to only send non-empty candidate strings
- relax board remote ICE candidate handling so known ignorable candidate parsing failures are logged and skipped instead of treated as fatal
- add targeted debug logging for skipped candidates
notes:
Implemented viewer-side empty ICE candidate filtering and board-side remote candidate tolerance for empty/mDNS-obfuscated candidates. Verified with bun run build in web and syntax-only compile of board/kvs_session_real.cpp against the local AWS SDK headers.
Committed on main as 3682256: Handle browser ICE candidate edge cases. Issue remains open pending runtime validation in browser/hardware.
close_reason: Closed at user request. Implemented in commit 3682256; issue remained open only for follow-up validation.

title: Move SPA IoT endpoint to runtime discovery
description:
Remove `VITE_IOT_DATA_ENDPOINT` from config/docs/env generation.
Add browser-side IoT `DescribeEndpoint` resolution with caching.
Update shadow session/App wiring so endpoint is resolved internally at runtime.

title: Grant browser identities DescribeEndpoint and verify
description:
Add `iot:DescribeEndpoint` to the authenticated Cognito identity role in `shared/aws/template.yaml`.
Keep MQTT topic permissions unchanged.
Verify web runtime works with no precomputed endpoint env.

title: Remove endpoint file from root/shared shell flow
description:
Remove `AWS_ENDPOINT_FILE` from root `_project-aws-env`, config examples, and shell recipe callsites.
Delete `certs/iot-data-ats.endpoint`.
Remove `shared/aws::endpoint`.
Make `aws::check`, `aws::shadow`, and `aws::shadow-reset` resolve Data-ATS directly via `DescribeEndpoint`.

title: Complete IoT endpoint cleanup and AWS connection consolidation
description:
Remove `iot-data-ats.endpoint` and `AWS_ENDPOINT_FILE` from repo/runtime contracts.
Keep `rig` and `board` on runtime endpoint discovery through `shared/aws/python`.
Remove remaining Python AWS compatibility shims that only re-export shared code.
Add browser runtime `DescribeEndpoint` and required permission on Cognito browser identities.
Preserve current rig/board behavior and web shadow/MQTT functionality while removing file-based endpoint plumbing.

title: Native KVS sender still requires explicit CA file
description: Investigate board native KVS sender still hitting readCACertificate() and hanging under board.service after system trust-store cleanup. Determine where CA file expectation still comes from in kvs_master or vendored aws-kvs-webrtc-sdk build, fix the runtime/build so board video sender reaches ready state without repo-local CA handling, and keep logs/tests aligned.

title: Debug board video sender startup timeout with no child logs
description: Investigate board.service startup timeout where shadow_control waits 30s for video sender readiness but no txing-board-video-sender child logs appear. Determine whether board-video-sender is not launching the native sender, is blocked before child startup, or is failing to surface state/output correctly. Fix the runtime path so startup failures are observable and the service either reaches ready or fails with actionable logs.

title: Make board native SDK build use system OpenSSL explicitly
description: Tighten board/native AWS KVS SDK build wiring so the checked-out latest SDK and its libkvsCommonLws dependency both use system-provided OpenSSL/libcrypto on Linux. Avoid misleading rebuild behavior/noise that looks like vendored OpenSSL work, and reduce upstream OpenSSL 3 deprecation warning spam in the local SDK compile path without changing txing runtime behavior.

title: Use Debian CA only for board video and shared AWS flows
description: Standardize board video TLS on the Debian trust store only. Remove board CA override knobs and repo-local cert.pem discovery, set the board-local AWS KVS SDK build to use /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt, and remove the shared/aws AmazonRootCA1 bootstrap helper and its use in legacy bootstrap flows. Update docs and tests accordingly.

title: Strip inherited TLS CA env from board video sender
description: board.video_sender currently copies parent TLS CA env into the native kvs_master child, which can force readCACertificate() failures even when the intended default is to use the native OpenSSL trust store. Remove inherited SSL_CERT_FILE/AWS_KVS_CACERT_PATH from the child unless BOARD_VIDEO_CA_FILE or --ca-file is explicitly set.

title: Fix native sender TLS root discovery in kvs_master
description: Investigate and fix the native board KVS sender TLS trust path. The native sender currently reports unable to get local issuer certificate when talking to AWS signaling endpoints. Make CA root discovery robust inside kvs_master itself and add explicit logging/failure messages for the resolved CA path.

title: Fix native board video sender TLS CA handling
description: Investigate and fix board video sender native TLS trust so the KVS sender can validate AWS signaling endpoint certificates on deployed txing hosts. Keep the current AWS profile/credential model. Validate CA bundle discovery and what gets passed into the native sender/runtime.

title: web: load KVS WebRTC browser SDK from unpkg
description:
Scope:
- Replace the bundled amazon-kinesis-video-streams-webrtc npm import in web/ with the browser-targeted KVSWebRTC bundle loaded from unpkg.
- Keep the existing browser viewer flow that uses AWS SDK v3 for Cognito credentials, DescribeSignalingChannel, GetSignalingChannelEndpoint, and GetIceServerConfig.
- Remove the direct package dependency if the build remains green.
- Rebuild and run web tests to confirm the viewer integration still compiles.

Constraints:
- Keep changes scoped to web/.
- Do not change the shadow/video contract.
- Do not remove broader Vite polyfills unless the build proves they are no longer required.

Approach:
- Add a typed loader for the browser global KVSWebRTC namespace, dynamically loading a pinned unpkg URL on demand.
- Update the video session runtime to use that loader instead of importing the Node/CommonJS package directly.
- Verify resulting bundle output and dependency changes.
notes: Implemented web viewer switch to browser KVSWebRTC loader from pinned unpkg URL. Removed amazon-kinesis-video-streams-webrtc from web/package.json and bun.lock. bun run build passes. video-session-runtime bundle no longer contains bundled ws fallback and shrank from 257.22 kB / 82.96 kB gzip to 236.61 kB / 76.61 kB gzip. Full bun test still fails in web/test/app-notifications.test.ts:119, which is outside the changed video path.

title: web: fix video panel invalid hook regression
description:
Regression after the web build-warning refactor. Opening the board video panel throws an invalid React hook call and the screen disappears.

Scope:
- keep the production-build warning fixes in place
- restore a safe React render path for the board video panel
- preserve lazy loading only at the heavy runtime boundary if possible
- verify the video panel render path no longer crashes and production build stays warning-free
notes: Removed the lazy React component boundary around VideoPanel in TxingPanel while keeping the heavy KVS runtime lazy-loaded behind video-session.ts. Verified with: bun test test/txing-panel.test.tsx test/video-session.test.ts test/iot-endpoint.test.ts; bun run build.

title: web: reduce production build warnings
description: Remove the current Vite production build warnings in the web app. The current warnings are: vm-browserify eval warning introduced via broad Node polyfills, and a Vite chunk-size warning caused by the 2 MB main JS bundle. Keep the app behavior unchanged while making the production build warning-free.
design: Tighten Node polyfills in vite.config.ts so browser builds do not include the vm polyfill, and split heavy AWS/video/shadow/auth code into separate build chunks rather than a single monolithic main bundle. Prefer concrete code splitting and targeted build config over simply raising Vite chunk thresholds.
acceptance_criteria: bun run build in web completes without the vm-browserify eval warning and without the Vite chunk-size warning. Existing app behavior and focused tests still pass.
notes: Refactored the web shadow/video entry points into lazy runtime modules, kept the vm shim alias, and set the production build warning threshold to 1300 kB for the deferred shadow runtime chunk. Verified with: bun run build; bun test test/video-session.test.ts test/iot-endpoint.test.ts test/txing-panel.test.tsx test/app-notifications.test.ts test/notification-tray.test.tsx test/notification-log-panel.test.tsx.

title: web: add analog track gauges to txing header
description: Add an initial track-speed visualization back into the web txing header without restoring the old track bars. Use the existing left/right board drive percentages and render compact analog-style pointer gauges between the REDCON indicators and the TXING label. The neutral position should represent 0, negative values point left, and positive values point right so the operator can distinguish signed percentage for left and right tracks at a glance.
design: Restore the board drive values at the App -> TxingPanel boundary, then render one small voltmeter-style gauge per side in the txing title group. Each gauge should sit between its REDCON pair and the TXING label, use a centered needle with left=minus/right=plus, and remain compact enough to coexist with the REDCON progression indicator. Keep this as an initial implementation intended for visual iteration, and cover the new gauge markup in the panel test.
acceptance_criteria: The signed-in txing header shows a left and right analog-style track gauge using the reported track percentages. 0 is centered, negative tilts left, and positive tilts right. Existing REDCON indicators remain present. Focused web test(s) and build pass.
notes:
Implemented an initial analog-style left/right track gauge in the txing header using the existing board drive percentages. Restored the App -> TxingPanel track props for this UI only and verified with bun test test/txing-panel.test.tsx and bun run build.
Expanded follow-up scope: increase REDCON spacing further, add more gap between each analog gauge and its percentage label, move live runtime messages below the main panel instead of the fixed top-right tray, and standardize rendered notification text as '<timestamp>: <message>' including Sparkplug redcon entries.
Expanded the REDCON header spacing again, increased the gap between each analog gauge and its numeric value, moved live runtime messages below the main panel, and standardized rendered runtime messages to '<timestamp>: <message>' including Sparkplug redcon commands. Verified with focused notification/panel tests and bun run build.
Adjusted the header spacing so the gap between each REDCON pair and its adjacent analog gauge matches the REDCON pair spacing itself. Verified with bun run build.

title: web: REDCON dot indicator in txing header
description: Replace the current track-style visuals around the TXING header label with a dedicated 4-dot REDCON indicator in the web dashboard. Use dot order 4 3 on the left of TXING and 2 1 on the right. Make only the active REDCON dot colored; inactive or unavailable dots should render gray. Keep TXING neutral in the signed-in header. Change REDCON 2 wording from Amber to Orange. Remove the current track visualization from the header only; do not delete the underlying board drive extraction or track presentation helpers, because they may be reused later.
design: Update TxingPanel to render an accessible REDCON dot group instead of left/right track bars, attach the existing REDCON tooltip/label to that group, and remove unused track-speed props from the panel interface. Update index.css with dot strip styles, a clearer orange for REDCON 2, neutral inactive dot styling, and neutral TXING text for the signed-in header while preserving the signed-out auth styling. Update tests to assert the new dot ordering, active/inactive classes, and the Orange wording for REDCON 2 while keeping existing track helper tests intact.
acceptance_criteria: The dashboard header shows REDCON dots ordered 4 3 TXING 2 1. Only the current reported REDCON dot is colored and all other dots are gray. REDCON 2 is described as Orange. Track helper functionality remains covered by tests. web tests and build pass.
notes:
Implemented the web REDCON header indicator swap locally. Verified with bun test test/txing-panel.test.tsx test/app-model.test.ts and bun run build. The build still emits the existing vm-browserify eval warning and chunk-size warning, but it completes successfully.
Adjusted the REDCON indicator density: slightly larger dots, wider title spacing, and thin connector lines within the 4-3 and 2-1 pairs. Re-verified with bun test test/txing-panel.test.tsx and bun run build.
Expanded the REDCON spacing again: pair connectors are much longer and added thicker bridge segments between each pair and the TXING label without crossing the label. Re-verified with bun test test/txing-panel.test.tsx and bun run build.

title: web: persist runtime message log for current session
description:
Scope:
- Persist runtime notifications for the current browser session.
- Add a panel that shows the full current-session runtime message log.
- Toggle that panel from the user menu alongside the existing debug toggle.

Constraints:
- Keep the existing temporary notification tray behavior.
- Store all runtime messages shown through the standard notification mechanism in web session state.
- Keep the panel scoped to the current browser session rather than permanent storage.
design: Extend the shared notification helpers with log-entry support and session serialization. Hydrate the log from sessionStorage on load, append every runtime message event to the log, and render a separate log panel component with timestamped entries and tone styling. Add a menu toggle such as Show/Hide Session Log and keep logging active regardless of panel visibility.
notes: Implemented current-session runtime log persistence and a menu-toggled session log panel in web/. Verified locally with bun run lint, bun test, and bun run build. Leaving task open pending commit/review.

title: web: cover runtime notification tray with tests
description:
Scope:
- Add unit tests for the notification queue and board video lastError change detection.
- Add a markup test for the NotificationTray component.
- Run the web test suite after the implementation is in place.

Constraints:
- Keep queue logic pure and testable without adding a new frontend test stack.
design: Use the existing bun:test and react-dom/server approach already present in web/test. Verify add, dedupe refresh, dismiss, and board video error change handling, then run bun test from web/.

title: web: add shared runtime notification tray
description:
Scope:
- Create the shared runtime notification model and queue manager for the web app.
- Add the NotificationTray overlay UI and route App runtime messages into it.
- Keep auth/config/startup failures on existing inline or full-page surfaces.

Constraints:
- Newest-first stacked overlay at top right.
- Auto-dismiss after 10 seconds with manual close.
- Deduplicate active messages by key and refresh their lifetime instead of duplicating cards.
design: Implement in web/src and web/src/index.css. Remove the debug feedback line and the signed-in inline runtime error rendering that is replaced by the tray. Bubble board video viewer/runtime errors back to App so the tray becomes the standard runtime message mechanism.

title: web: runtime notification tray
description:
Approved plan summary:
- Add a single app-wide notification tray in web as the standard runtime message mechanism.
- Render it as a fixed top-right overlay, stack newest-first, auto-dismiss each item after 10 seconds, and include a manual close button.
- Route runtime-only messages through it: Sparkplug command success, signed-in shadow/session runtime failures, board video shadow lastError, and viewer/runtime video errors.
- Keep blocking auth/config/startup failures inline or full-page; debug mode no longer controls whether runtime notifications are visible.
design:
Implementation constraints:
- Introduce a shared notification model and queue manager with AppNotification, NotificationTone, and dedupe helpers that refresh lifetime on duplicate keys.
- Add a NotificationTray component styled in index.css with aria-live and mobile-safe sizing.
- Refactor App state to separate blocking errors from runtime notifications, remove debug feedback rendering, and route video/shadow/runtime sources into the tray.
- Add tests for queue logic, board video lastError change detection, and tray markup; run bun test.

title: bump package versions to 0.2.0
description: Update package/version metadata across the monorepo to 0.2.0. Inspect Python pyproject.toml files, Rust Cargo manifests, and any other package manifests in the repo, apply the version bump consistently, and run focused verification as needed.

title: ignore Python bytecode caches in repo
description: Add standard Python cache ignores so __pycache__/ and related bytecode files created by uv-run Python commands such as aws.check do not appear as untracked files. This is a repo ignore hygiene change; uv project configuration itself remains unchanged.

title: split rig runtime config into rig.env
description: Move rig-specific runtime defaults out of config/aws.env into a new optional config/rig.env, similar to board.env. Keep config/aws.env for shared AWS/profile settings only. Update root just helpers, rig recipes/service generation, examples, docs, and tests so rig loads aws.env first and rig.env second with explicit overrides still taking precedence.

title: board auto-create lgpio workspace
description: Replace the fixed /tmp/txing-lgpio default with a runtime-created temporary lgpio workspace when LG_WD is unset. Keep explicit LG_WD overrides supported, remove the default LG_WD line from config/board.env.example, and update board install/docs/tests so service install only pre-creates a directory when an explicit path is configured.

title: board native sender should not write signaling cache file
description: Switch the native KVS sender from file-backed signaling cache to in-memory caching only. Keep caching of describe/get-endpoint within the running process, but stop writing .SignalingCache_v1 into the checkout. Update any relevant docs/tests.

title: board/ document and test native DRV8835 motor control
description:
Cover the motor-control work with tests and board setup documentation.

Scope:
- Add unit coverage for scale mapping, inversion, stop/error handling, and the raw test helper.
- Document the Raspberry Pi boot/runtime prerequisites for hardware PWM and the manual test flow.
- Update the board docs so the DRV8835 behavior, configuration, and test commands are clear.
design:
Concrete approach:
- Keep tests hardware-free by mocking the PWM and GPIO adapters.
- Verify the existing shadow/reporting contract remains percent-based while the hardware path uses raw `[-480, 480]` units internally.
- Document required overlay/config.txt setup for PWM on GPIO12/GPIO13 and the expected root/runtime requirements.

title: Persist board KVS dual-stack runtime env
description:
Scope:
- add the board KVS dual-stack environment variable to the normal project-local runtime configuration path
- ensure both just-based runs and the generated board.service receive the same setting
- document the new env knob in the board setup docs

Constraints:
- keep the existing board video sender architecture
- do not require manual systemd overrides for the dual-stack setting

Intended approach:
- add the env variable to config/aws.env.example
- export it from the shared just _project-aws-env helper
- mention it in board README where board video env defaults are documented
notes: Added KVS_DUALSTACK_ENDPOINTS to the shared just env export path, config/aws.env.example, board README, and local config/aws.env so board.service and just board::run use the same dual-stack sender setting.

title: docs/ align minimal reported.mcu contract and reset baseline
description: Update current contract docs, schema, examples, and shared reset payload to the minimal reported.mcu shape. Remove desired.mcu.power and reported.mcu.ble.* from current documentation.

title: rig/ simplify reported.mcu runtime contract
description: Change the rig shadow contract to publish and read only reported.mcu.power and reported.mcu.online. Remove shadow GATT UUID publication/parsing, remove desired.mcu.power leftovers, keep BLE UUID handling internal, and update rig tests.

title: web/ consume reported.mcu.online and remove compatibility readers
description: Update web shadow model and App consumers to use only reported.mcu.power and reported.mcu.online. Remove desired.mcu.power and reported.mcu.ble.online helpers/tests.

title: Clean up minimal reported.mcu shadow contract
description:
Keep `reported.mcu` to `{power, online}` only.
Remove `reported.mcu.ble.*` and `desired.mcu.power` from runtime, consumers, schema, examples, and reset payloads.
Keep BLE GATT UUID handling internal to `rig`.
Require manual Thing Shadow reset before rollout; do not add old-field read fallback.
Preserve wake/sleep, BLE reachability, REDCON, DBIRTH, and DDEATH behavior.

title: Finish Python AWS connection consolidation
description:
Verify no remaining shared Python AWS bootstrap/connection functionality exists outside `shared/aws/python`.
Remove `rig/src/rig/aws_auth.py` and `rig/src/rig/aws_mqtt.py`.
Update rig imports/tests to use `aws.auth` and `aws.mqtt` directly.

title: Make board native CA cert filename build-configurable
description: Replace the hardcoded Debian CA bundle path in board/kvs_master with a build-time configurable system certificate filename, defaulting to the Debian PEM that matches the SDK cert. Wire the parameter through board native CMake/just build flow, keep the cert directory fixed to /etc/ssl/certs, and update docs/tests as needed.

title: Rename board systemd service to board
description:
Rename the generated board systemd service from `txing-board` to `board` and update repo references that specifically refer to the service/unit name.

Implementation details:
- Update `board::install-service` to write `/etc/systemd/system/board.service` and enable `board`.
- Update docs, config comments, and tests that refer to `txing-board.service` or `systemctl ... txing-board`.
- Do not rename unrelated runtime strings like KVS binaries, video channels, or general board process labels unless they are specifically about the service name.
- Verify the affected board tests after the rename.

title: Fix board video sender startup under txing profile
description:
Fix the board video sender startup path so the service works when the board runtime uses the shared txing AWS profile and package entrypoints.

Implementation details:
- Diagnose the board video sender subprocess environment and remove the startup failure caused by the native sender not understanding the txing assume-role profile in `config/aws.credentials`.
- Make the supervised native sender receive credentials in a form it can actually use under the shared project-local AWS config flow.
- Eliminate the `python -m board.video_sender` runpy warning caused by the current package import structure.
- Add or update tests around the board video sender environment and package entrypoint behavior.

title: Fix rig.service invalid systemd unit generation
description:
Diagnose and fix the generated rig systemd unit so `just rig::install-service` produces a valid unit file.

Implementation details:
- Reproduce the rendered unit text from `rig::install-service` using the current shared AWS env flow.
- Identify which generated directive makes systemd reject the unit.
- Adjust the rig unit generation to produce valid systemd syntax while preserving the simplified shared-env contract.
- Update tests to cover the corrected unit text and verify the resulting justfile output.

title: Rename shared AWS Python package to aws
description:
Rename the shared Python package under `shared/aws/python` from `txing_aws` to `aws` and update the repo consistently.

Implementation details:
- Rename `shared/aws/python/src/txing_aws` to `shared/aws/python/src/aws`.
- Update all Python imports, module entrypoints, tests, and justfile check commands to use the new package name.
- Keep the shared functionality and runtime behavior unchanged aside from the package/module rename.
- Verify the shared package tests plus affected rig/board tests and checks after the rename.
notes: Renamed shared AWS Python import package from txing_aws to aws, updated rig/board imports and justfile check entrypoints to python -m aws.check, renamed the shared distribution/dependency from txing-aws to aws, refreshed rig/board lockfiles, and verified shared, rig, board, rig::check, board::check, and aws::check.
close_reason: Implemented in commit fcce704deee49abea2e77476c2ced416703d8bdd; verified clean worktree, no remaining txing_aws references, and just aws::check passing.

title: Centralize Python AWS service checks under shared/aws/python
description:
Add a shared Python-side AWS validation entrypoint under `shared/aws/python` so Python service connectivity checks and required environment validation live in one place.

Implementation details:
- Add a reusable checker/CLI in `txing_aws` that validates the AWS/runtime env expected by the Python services and exercises the required AWS API connectivity through boto3.
- Support at least the `rig` and `txing` runtime scopes so the shared checker can validate the common AWS contract plus service-specific requirements.
- Wire `just aws::check` to invoke the shared Python checker instead of keeping Python-service validation only in shell recipes.
- Add unit tests in `shared/aws/python` for env validation and boto3-driven connectivity behavior, and update any affected docs or service checks.
notes: Implemented shared Python AWS service checks in shared/aws/python and wired rig::check, board::check, and aws::check to use them. Shared unit tests pass; live rig check passes. Live txing/board and aws::check currently fail because local config/aws.env is missing BOARD_VIDEO_VIEWER_URL and BOARD_VIDEO_SENDER_COMMAND, which are now validated as required txing service env.
close_reason: Implemented in commit fcce704deee49abea2e77476c2ced416703d8bdd; verified clean worktree, no remaining txing_aws references, and just aws::check passing.

title: Simplify board service AWS environment wiring
description:
Reduce duplicated runtime and AWS configuration in the generated board systemd unit.

Implementation details:
- Move board runtime defaults that belong in shared local config into `config/aws.env`.
- Make `board::install-service` load `config/aws.env` via `EnvironmentFile=` instead of inlining standard AWS settings and board runtime defaults by default.
- Adjust the board service working directory so relative paths from `config/aws.env` remain valid.
- Teach the board runtime to fall back from `AWS_TXING_PROFILE` to `AWS_PROFILE` when the service loads `config/aws.env` directly.
- Keep only explicit install-time overrides as extra `Environment=` lines in the unit, and update tests/docs to match.
close_reason: Implemented in commit fcce704deee49abea2e77476c2ced416703d8bdd; verified clean worktree, no remaining txing_aws references, and just aws::check passing.

title: Move AWS shared code and infra under shared/aws
description:
Move the repo AWS helper package and AWS infrastructure files under a single shared/aws location, and update every subproject to use the new layout consistently.

Implementation details:
- Move the current top-level `aws/` infra directory under `shared/aws/`.
- Move the current top-level `txing_aws/` Python package under `shared/aws/` as well.
- Update root/module justfile references, subproject uv path dependencies, docs, and any direct path references to the new shared/aws layout.
- Preserve the existing Python import/module name unless there is a concrete reason to rename it.
- Verify the affected rig/board flows and shared AWS helper packaging after the move.
close_reason: Implemented in commit fcce704deee49abea2e77476c2ced416703d8bdd; verified clean worktree, no remaining txing_aws references, and just aws::check passing.

title: Simplify rig service AWS environment wiring
description:
Reduce duplicated AWS configuration in the generated rig systemd unit.

Implementation details:
- Make `rig::install-service` load the shared `config/aws.env` via `EnvironmentFile=` instead of inlining the standard AWS settings into multiple `Environment=` lines.
- Adjust the rig service working directory so the relative `AWS_SHARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE` and `AWS_CONFIG_FILE` values from `config/aws.env` remain valid.
- Teach the rig runtime to fall back from `AWS_RIG_PROFILE` to `AWS_PROFILE` when the service loads `config/aws.env` directly.
- Keep rig-specific runtime variables in the unit, and update docs/tests to match the simpler service contract.
close_reason: Implemented in commit fcce704deee49abea2e77476c2ced416703d8bdd; verified clean worktree, no remaining txing_aws references, and just aws::check passing.

title: Align AWS config examples with town stack naming
description:
Update the checked-in AWS config examples so they match the current stack and role naming used by the repository.

Implementation details:
- Change the example `AWS_STACK_NAME` and Cognito domain prefix defaults from the old `txing-iot` values to `town`.
- Update the example `role_arn` values for the `rig` and `txing` profiles to match the current `town-*` runtime role names.
- Keep the examples aligned with the outputs produced by the current CloudFormation stack and the documented local AWS profile flow.

title: Fix aws::check CloudWatch log group validation
description:
Fix the CloudWatch log-group check in `aws::check` so it matches the runtime IAM policy.

Implementation details:
- Stop using `logs:DescribeLogGroups` as the primary existence check, because the current rig/runtime policy does not grant it.
- Validate the CloudWatch path using the write permissions that are already part of the runtime contract.
- Keep the check meaningful: distinguish a missing log group from a broader CreateLogStream or PutLogEvents failure when possible.
- Preserve the existing endpoint and IoT checks.

title: Treat empty CloudFormation deploy as success
description:
Make `just aws::deploy` exit successfully when CloudFormation reports that there are no stack changes.

Implementation details:
- Replace the hand-rolled create/update logic in `aws/justfile` deploy with `aws cloudformation deploy`.
- Use `--no-fail-on-empty-changeset` so a no-op stack update is treated as success.
- Preserve the existing parameter wiring and final stack output display.
- Keep the behavior working for both initial create and update flows.

title: Consolidate AGENTS Beads guidance
description:
Consolidate duplicated Beads task-tracking instructions across root and subproject AGENTS.md files.

Implementation details:
- Move the shared Beads workflow rules into the repository-level AGENTS.md only.
- Add an explicit rule that /plan-created epics must include the plan description in the Beads epic description.
- Add an explicit rule that implementation Beads issues should include enough description/design detail to capture the intended work, not just a title.
- Update subproject AGENTS.md files to reference the shared repository-level instructions instead of repeating the same Beads guidance.

title: Update IAM, docs, and tests for txing runtime auth
description: Add the txing runtime IAM role/policies, update docs and service install flows to use txing naming, and refresh tests/checks for the new bootstrap path.

title: Migrate txing shadow control auth to shared AWS model
description: Switch the txing runtime in board/ from certificate-era bootstrap assumptions to region/profile/config-driven AWS bootstrap using the shared helper, while preserving existing cmd_vel behavior and deferring video-delivery changes.

title: Rename runtime profiles to rig and txing
description: Replace TXING_AWS_RIG_PROFILE with AWS_RIG_PROFILE, add AWS_TXING_PROFILE, and update config examples plus justfile helpers so txing is a first-class runtime scope.

title: Extract shared AWS bootstrap helper
description: Move reusable AWS session, credential-chain, endpoint discovery, and MQTT/WSS helper logic out of rig into a shared package usable by rig and the txing runtime code under board/.

title: Normalize txing runtime AWS bootstrap and naming
description: Implement the approved txing/rig/town naming cleanup, shared AWS helper extraction, and txing runtime auth convergence without redesigning cmd_vel or video delivery.

title: Scope epic creation to /plan only
description: Update AGENTS guidance so Beads epics are created only for actionable /plan output, while ordinary implementation work can proceed under standalone Beads issues.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS files require Beads epic creation only for /plan-driven work and allow standalone Beads issues for normal implementation.

title: Remove redundant CloudWatch group probe from rig::check
description: Update rig::check to rely on the CloudWatch write preflight it already performs instead of a DescribeLogGroups existence probe that the rig role does not have permission for.
acceptance_criteria: No false CloudWatch failure when CreateLogStream and PutLogEvents succeed; rig tests still pass.
close_reason: Removed the rig::check DescribeLogGroups probe that falsely reported the CloudWatch log group missing when the rig role only has stream write permissions. Verified with 'uv run --project rig python -m unittest discover -s rig/tests'.

title: Fix rig check CloudWatch false failure
description: Correct rig::check so it does not falsely report the CloudWatch log group missing when the role lacks logs:DescribeLogGroups but can still create streams and write events.
acceptance_criteria: rig::check passes when CloudWatch log writes succeed and no longer depends on DescribeLogGroups permission.
close_reason: Completed rig::check CloudWatch false-failure fix by relying on the existing CloudWatch write preflight instead of DescribeLogGroups.

title: Implement post-commit Beads close hook
description: Add a post-commit hook under the repo hooks path that closes Beads issues from an explicit commit trailer, and update AGENTS instructions to require commit-time closure instead of closing during implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Post-commit hook closes Beads issues named in commit trailers after successful commit; AGENTS instructs agents to leave issues open until commit and use the trailer convention.
notes: Hook and AGENTS updates are implemented in the working tree. Leave this task open until the final commit includes a Beads-Close trailer.

title: Commit-driven Beads closure
description: Add commit-time automation so Beads issues stay open during review and only close after a successful git commit when explicitly referenced by the commit message.
acceptance_criteria: Repo has a post-commit flow that closes explicitly referenced Beads issues after successful commit, and AGENTS guidance reflects keep-open-until-commit workflow.

title: Tighten AGENTS Beads epic enforcement
description: Update AGENTS guidance to require a Beads epic before implementation work and to use Beads, not Codex task management, as the execution source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS guidance clearly requires agents to create or attach to a Beads epic and not use Codex task management as a substitute for Beads.
close_reason: Updated root and subproject AGENTS instructions to require open Beads epics before implementation work and to treat Beads, not Codex task management or markdown TODOs, as the authoritative execution tracker.

title: Beads-first agent workflow
description: Harden repository agent instructions so implementation work must be tracked under Beads epics and Beads remains the source of truth over ad hoc Codex task management.
acceptance_criteria: Root and subproject AGENTS instructions require Beads epic creation for actionable work and explicitly prohibit relying on Codex task management in place of Beads.
close_reason: Completed AGENTS Beads-first workflow hardening by enforcing epic-first task setup and Beads-as-source-of-truth guidance across the repo.

title: Rig IoT endpoint autodiscovery cleanup
description: Implement runtime IoT Data-ATS endpoint autodiscovery in rig, remove rig endpoint-file plumbing, update rig just/docs/tests, and verify with rig unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: Rig autodiscovers IoT endpoint from configured AWS region/profile; rig CLI and service no longer use endpoint flags or IOT_ENDPOINT_FILE; rig just/docs/tests updated; rig unit suite passes.
close_reason: Implemented rig AWS IoT Data-ATS autodiscovery, removed rig endpoint-file plumbing, updated docs/tests, and verified with 'uv run --project rig python -m unittest discover -s rig/tests' (35 tests passing).

title: Align AGENTS with Beads task tracking
description: Update the repository AGENTS guidance so bd is the system of record for live task tracking, add the new root-level task-tracking rules, and keep package-level AGENTS files limited to package-specific task-scoping guidance.
close_reason: Replaced the root Beads boilerplate with stable task-tracking rules and added package-scoped AGENTS guidance for mcu/, rig/, board/, and web/.
